controls.value: RH upper lane; LH lower lane; middle lane redistributed between hands only where reachable

sections.name: Hammer Gate
journey: raw repeated-note motor opens into A-minor/F/E7 keyboard territory
destination: the motor has a clear pitch identity and a pickup into the toccata statement

harmony: A minor: raw motor, F pressure, E7 turnback
process: register opens in cascades, but the torrent stays lighter than the later S6 density maximum

checks: first bar checkpoint for A minor: raw motor, F pressure, E7 turnback

gestures.idea: A minor: raw motor, F pressure, E7 turnback
mechanism: upper, middle, and lower piano lanes enter at the same bar with explicit event material

line_relations.text: foreground register is heard against the notated bass-line timing

harmony: D minor to A/E, F, E7, pickup into theme
process: upper accents run in dotted-quarter cells against the lower quarter motor; this is the audible return dissonance

checks: first bar checkpoint for D minor to A/E, F, E7, pickup into theme

sections.name: Toccata Statement
journey: the motor becomes a real theme over bass-as-line motion
destination: the cadence is denied into a fugato pickup rather than boxed off

harmony: A minor theme: Am, C, F, E7
process: theme, bass, and inner-harmony engines exchange weight inside each bar; the A head is octave-bright so later returns are recognizable

checks: first bar checkpoint for A minor theme: Am, C, F, E7

gestures.idea: A minor theme: Am, C, F, E7
mechanism: upper, middle, and lower piano lanes enter at the same bar with explicit event material

line_relations.text: foreground register is heard against the notated bass-line timing

harmony: three-bar variation turn: Am, G, F without a boxed cadence
process: the variation turn interrupts its own running pattern with rests and chordal echoes before the next harmony arrives

checks: first bar checkpoint for three-bar variation turn: Am, G, F without a boxed cadence

harmony: five-bar fragmentation: Dm, Am/C, Bdim, E7 stretched across the old seam
process: fragmentation crosses the old span seam: each bar borrows the next bar's rhythmic engine before the harmony turns

checks: first bar checkpoint for five-bar fragmentation: Dm, Am/C, Bdim, E7 stretched across the old seam

harmony: Am, F, Dm-E7, A cadence denied into C
process: the denied cadence keeps fragments of the running bass while the upper line falls out of phase with it

checks: first bar checkpoint for Am, F, Dm-E7, A cadence denied into C

sections.name: Mirror Fugato
journey: theme and countersubject split across hands in staggered counterpoint
destination: the counterpoint compresses into chord/rest blows that prepare the lock

harmony: A-theme over C answer; G/B pressure; countersubject starts the departure
process: A keeps its octave-bright head while the B fence enters tenuto in the middle register before the previous idea has cleared

checks: first bar checkpoint for A-theme over C answer; G/B pressure; countersubject starts the departure

gestures.idea: A-theme over C answer; G/B pressure; countersubject starts the departure
mechanism: upper, middle, and lower piano lanes enter at the same bar with explicit event material

line_relations.text: foreground register is heard against the notated bass-line timing

harmony: F and C/E answers compress toward E7, with entries offset rather than cadential
process: the high answer, middle answer, and bass answer overlap by half-bars so the compression does not reset every measure

checks: first bar checkpoint for F and C/E answers compress toward E7, with entries offset rather than cadential

harmony: Am/F/Dm/E7 fragments squeezed into pre-lock imitation
process: the bass alternates line and pillar inside the same span while upper fragments begin to harden into chord attacks

checks: first bar checkpoint for Am/F/Dm/E7 fragments squeezed into pre-lock imitation

harmony: C/Bdim/E7 pressure collapses into A lock preparation
process: the chordal lock begins early as inserted blows, then leaves enough running residue to avoid a square handoff

checks: first bar checkpoint for C/Bdim/E7 pressure collapses into A lock preparation

sections.name: Vertical Lock
journey: the running motor is crushed into chordal-rhythmic clauses
destination: the midpoint formal destination: A-minor pressure speaks as one vertical instrument

harmony: Am/F/Dm/E7 vertical sentence, secco and non-repeatable
process: the vertical lock stays intentionally chordal, but accents and gaps keep it from becoming a copied four-bar grid

checks: first bar checkpoint for Am/F/Dm/E7 vertical sentence, secco and non-repeatable

gestures.idea: Am/F/Dm/E7 vertical sentence, secco and non-repeatable
mechanism: upper, middle, and lower piano lanes enter at the same bar with explicit event material

line_relations.text: foreground register is heard against the notated bass-line timing

harmony: Am, Dm-to-E7, F, E7 martellato clauses answer the fugato collapse
process: martellato clauses answer the previous lock with displaced rests and premature cadential weight

checks: first bar checkpoint for Am/C/G/F/E7 martellato clauses answer the fugato collapse

harmony: Dm/E7/Am release seam breaks open rather than cadencing softly
process: the release seam breaks the lock open with melody above pillars, preparing the torrent before the section line

checks: first bar checkpoint for Dm/E7/Am release seam breaks open rather than cadencing softly

sections.name: Register Torrent
journey: the locked keyboard breaks open into wide spatial cascades and exposed high register
destination: the high point and extreme-register map make the keyboard larger after the lock

harmony: F/C/Dm aftershock pivots into committed B-flat minor at bar 56
process: the cascade is interrupted by pillars, then every lane pivots into B-flat minor together instead of sprinkling isolated color notes

checks: first bar checkpoint for F/C/E/Dm/A aftershock opens the keyboard spatially

gestures.idea: F/C/E/Dm/A aftershock opens the keyboard spatially
mechanism: upper cascade is reduced to eighth-note sweep while bass and middle hold larger pillars

line_relations.text: foreground opens space without matching the later S6 density

harmony: E-flat minor to F-sharp major, B major, and E major: each bar commits all lanes to the new key
process: the remote color is no longer a gap effect; every hand carries each key before the next rapid shift

checks: first bar checkpoint for E-flat minor to F-sharp major, B major, and E major

harmony: C-sharp minor, F-sharp major, and B major complete the rapid-key passage before bar 64 turns out
process: bars 61-63 remain in committed remote keys across all hands; only bar 64 uses E7/A as the exit

checks: first bar checkpoint for C-sharp minor, F-sharp major, and B major rapid-key passage

harmony: A/Dm/E7/A fragments tighten toward the polyrhythmic return
process: return fragments tighten into the polyrhythmic engine before the next section formally begins

checks: first bar checkpoint for A/Dm/E7/A fragments tighten toward the polyrhythmic return

sections.name: Polyrhythmic Return
journey: the A melody returns plainly, but with a final-stage bass engine and harmonic middle shadow before the density maximum rebuilds
destination: three-deck texture buckles into compressed coda preparation

harmony: A-theme recall: Am, C, F-to-E7, A
process: the A head is stated plainly, then intensified by a harmonic middle shadow and an active low-register bass engine instead of a copied first-statement accompaniment

checks: first bar checkpoint for A-theme recall: Am, C, F-to-E7, A

gestures.idea: A-theme recall: Am, C, F-to-E7, A
mechanism: the upper line states the A melody plainly while the middle shadows it in harmony and the bass intensifies into a low eighth-note engine

line_relations.text: the middle line reinforces the theme below the foreground instead of replacing it

harmony: inner melody carries Dm/Am/C/Bdim/E7 while the low motor persists
process: density thickens beat by beat, but the B return is projected as a marked middle-register melody

checks: first bar checkpoint for inner melody carries Dm/Am/C/Bdim/E7 while the low motor persists

harmony: Am/F/Dm/E7 controlled crest with displaced upper accents
process: the crest keeps the polyrhythmic contradiction but backs away from constant hammered maximum

checks: first bar checkpoint for Am/F/Dm/E7 controlled crest with displaced upper accents

gestures.idea: Am/F/Dm/E7 controlled crest with displaced upper accents
mechanism: low motor stays in small attacks while upper accents stretch over three-quarter spans and middle carries the melody without constant maximum dynamics

line_relations.text: upper displacement is audible against the low motor rather than only named in prose

harmony: A/F/E7/A compression relaxes the crest before the final coda
process: coda compression begins as interruptions inside the return, with reduced attack weight so the ending can still speak

checks: first bar checkpoint for A/F/E7/A compression relaxes the crest before the final coda

sections.name: Hard-Cut Coda
journey: the lock returns as a clarified final A-minor/E7 claim with one last plain A-theme recall
destination: the final A-axis strike cuts off rather than dissolving

harmony: compressed A claim spends earlier material while preparing the final theme recall
process: the block texture is lighter than the crest and leaves room for the near-end A statement to register

checks: first bar checkpoint for compressed A claim prepares the final theme recall

gestures.idea: compressed A claim prepares the final theme recall
mechanism: right hand states compact chord shapes while left hand carries low A/F/D/E pillars without constant maximum attacks

line_relations.text: coda blocks are playable as separated hand roles, not duplicated bass in the upper lane

harmony: final A-theme recall over restrained Am/C/F/E7 support
process: the A melody returns plainly near the end, supported by harmonic shadow and steady bass instead of block-chord pounding

checks: first bar checkpoint for final A-theme recall over restrained Am/C/F/E7 support

gestures.idea: final A-theme recall over restrained Am/C/F/E7 support
mechanism: upper lane states the theme while the middle shadows it and the bass supports without block-chord doubling

line_relations.text: the inner line reinforces the theme instead of replacing the foreground

harmony: Dm/E7/A final seal and cut
process: the final seal alternates impact and afterimage so the ending feels cut off, not merely stopped after four block bars

checks: first bar checkpoint for Dm/E7/A final seal and cut

gestures.idea: Dm/E7/A final seal and cut
mechanism: final low strike is left-hand bass only while right hand seals upper A and E-register chord shapes

line_relations.text: hand separation remains clear through the final cut
